arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which symchk 2>/dev/null; which debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null
arguments.description: Check for .NET SDK and symbol tools

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check for .NET SDK and symbol tools

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The tombstone shows frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with a Build ID of `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at various PC offsets but only have raw addresses, not function names.

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific Build ID
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar to map addresses to function names


3. The Build ID corresponds to a specific .NET SDK version, and I can try fetching the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols` using the Build ID, then use `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses to function names.

Let me check what debugging tools are available on this system.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5
which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which symchk 2>/dev/null; which debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null
arguments.description: Check for .NET SDK and symbol tools

arguments.command:
# Try to fetch the symbol file from Microsoft's symbol server using the Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try different URL patterns
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""
arguments.description: Fetch symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Fetch symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available and `dotnet`. Now I need to get the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

Microsoft publishes symbols on their symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`. The format for fetching symbols by Build ID (ELF) is typically:

`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/libmonosgen-2.0.so`


Or I could try the debug file path instead. Let me attempt to fetch the symbols using the Build ID in the Microsoft symbol server URL format for ELF binaries.

arguments.command:
# Try to fetch the symbol file from Microsoft's symbol server using the Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try different URL patterns
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""
arguments.description: Fetch symbols from Microsoft symbol server
